vulnerability.title: Add nosuid Option to /boot
vulnerability.description: The nosuid mount option can be used to prevent execution of setuid programs in /boot. The SUID and SGID permissions should not be required on the boot partition. Add the nosuid option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /boot has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/boot")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/boot' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: Adding the nosuid mount option to /boot prevents execution of setuid programs in the boot partition, thereby reducing privilege escalation risk.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; privilege_escalation; unauthorized_execution

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for mount_option_boot_nosuid (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_mount_option_boot_nosuid). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
```python
def remediate_mount_option_boot_nosuid():
    # Read the /etc/fstab file
    lines_result = read_file(path='/etc/fstab')
    if is_error(lines_result):
        return lines_result
    
    lines = lines_result
    new_lines = []
    
    for line in lines:
        # Skip comment lines and empty lines
        if line.startswith('#') or line.strip() == '':
            new_lines.append(line)
            continue
            
        # Split the line into words by whitespace
        words = line.split()
        # Ensure we have at least 6 words by padding with empty strings if necessary
        if len(words) < 6:
            words.extend([''] * (6 - len(words)))
        # Take exactly the first 6 words
        words = words[:6]
        
        # Unpack the six fields
        device, mount_point, fstype, mntops, freq, passno = words[0], words[1], words[2], words[3], words[4], words[5]
        
        # Check if this line is for the /boot mount point
        if mount_point.strip() == '/boot':
            # Process the mount options (mntops) field
            if mntops.strip() == '':
                # If mntops is empty, set it to 'nosuid'
                mntops_modified = 'nosuid'
            else:
                # Split mntops by commas, strip each token, and remove empty tokens
                tokens = [t.strip() for t in mntops.split(',') if t.strip() != '']
                # If 'nosuid' is not already present, add it
                if 'nosuid' not in tokens:
                    tokens.append('nosuid')
                # Rejoin the tokens with commas to form the new mntops string
                mntops_modified = ','.join(tokens)
            
            # Construct the new line with the modified mntops field
            new_line = f"{device} {mount_point} {fstype} {mntops_modified} {freq} {passno}"
            new_lines.append(new_line)
        else:
            # If not the /boot mount point, keep the line as is
            new_lines.append(line)
    
    # Write the modified content back to /etc/fstab
    content = '\n'.join(new_lines)
    write_result = write_file(path='/etc/fstab', content=content)
    if is_error(write_result):
        return write_result
    
    # Return success
    return {'success': True}
```
